subject: [CMPv2-CERT-PROVIDER] Add helm chart for K8s external provider
commit_message:
[CMPv2-CERT-PROVIDER] Add helm chart for K8s external provider

Cert Service K8s external provider ia a part of certificate distribution infrastructure in ONAP.
The main functionality of the provider is to forward Certificate Signing Requests (CSRs) created by cert-mananger (https://cert-manager.io) to CertServiceAPI.

More information can found on a dedicated page:  https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/CertService+and+K8s+Cert-Manager+integration.

Issue-ID: OOM-2560
Signed-off-by: Jan Malkiewicz <jan.malkiewicz@nokia.com>
Change-Id: Ibc94d5db5cac9649d47143406b47ce179beddd14

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-2

You should use certInitializer API and your service as one of possible backends to minimize the amount of changes that we need to do in project charts

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review-2
> 
> You should use certInitializer API and your service as one of possible backends to minimize the amount of changes that we need to do in project charts

Could explain me in more details what you exactly mean?

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> > Patch Set 3: Code-Review-2
> > 
> > You should use certInitializer API and your service as one of possible backends to minimize the amount of changes that we need to do in project charts
> 
> Could explain me in more details what you exactly mean?

Right. After reading this commit for a second time I'm not sure that I understand your intentions correctly at the first time.

So our idea while introducing certInitializer was to use it as an universal API for the projects to obtain certificate no matter what kind of "backend" is being used.

So we imagined that certInitializer will have at least 2 backends AAF and cert-manager so personally I expected that we could do pretty much the same for cmpv2. So we imagined that cmpv2 support could be added to cert-manager and then user would choose which issuer should be used for his onap deployment

> > > You should use certInitializer API and your service as one of possible backends to minimize the amount of changes that we need to do in project charts
> > 
> > Could explain me in more details what you exactly mean?
> 
> Right. After reading this commit for a second time I'm not sure that I understand your intentions correctly at the first time.
> 
> So our idea while introducing certInitializer was to use it as an universal API for the projects to obtain certificate no matter what kind of "backend" is being used.
> 
> So we imagined that certInitializer will have at least 2 backends AAF and cert-manager so personally I expected that we could do pretty much the same for cmpv2. So we imagined that cmpv2 support could be added to cert-manager and then user would choose which issuer should be used for his onap deployment


@Krzysztof: oom-k8s-external-provider is a Cert-Manager's external issuer which uses CMPv2 service to enroll certificates from CMPv2 server. As such it is a new K8s workload which reconciles CertManager's CertificateRequest as well as our custom CRD - CMPv2Issuer. In fact, our external provider is an implementation of K8s operator... 
More details can be found on https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/CertService+and+K8s+Cert-Manager+integration 

We are planning to extend certInitilizer API by new template which will create CertManager's Certificate resource which could be used by all other helm charts which need certificate from CMPv2 server...
